sanskrit:
बहूनि मे व्यतीतानि जन्मानि तव चार्जुन।
तान्यहं वेद सर्वाणि न त्वं वेत्थ परन्तप।।
transliteration:
bahūni me vyatītāni janmāni tava cārjuna |
tāny ahaṃ veda sarvāṇi na tvaṃ vettha parantapa ||
literal_meaning: Many births of Mine have passed, and also of yours, O Arjuna; all of them I know, but you do not know them, O scorcher of foes.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reminds Arjuna that while both have lived countless lives, only Krishna retains full memory; Arjuna’s amnesia illustrates the ordinary human condition of dissociation from past experience and identity.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance: Arjuna confronts the gap between his limited self-narrative and the vast continuity of his soul, evoking a therapeutic moment of ego dissolution.
trigger_scenario: During therapy, a client recalls a past-life memory or childhood trauma that rewrites their life story, triggering both relief and vertigo.
anchor_text: Remember, your soul has lived before, even if your mind forgets.
